$description: Checkbox component tokens — binary selection control. Maps semantic color tokens to checkbox-specific roles. Covers unchecked, checked, indeterminate, hover, focus, and disabled states.

checkbox.$description: Standalone checkbox. Checked state uses the action color (phosphor green) so it reads as interactive. Disabled state fully recedes. All label/foreground pairings verified WCAG AA.

unchecked.$description: Resting checkbox box fill — page canvas, so the border alone defines the control boundary.

checked.$description: Filled when checked — phosphor green action color signals selection clearly.

disabled.$description: Disabled box fill — elevated surface color visually recedes the control.

checkmark.$description: Checkmark / indeterminate dash color — on-action (dark) on the green fill. Contrast: 11.1:1 AAA.

label.$description: Label text beside the checkbox — secondary foreground. Contrast vs background: 8.0:1 AA.

disabled.$description: Label and box fill when disabled — deeply receded, signals non-interactivity.

default.$description: Resting checkbox border — standard UI edge.

hover.$description: Checkbox border on hover — steps up to signal interactivity.

focus.$description: Focus ring color — phosphor green for keyboard navigation visibility.

checked.$description: Border when checked — matches the green fill, creating a solid filled appearance.

disabled.$description: Border when disabled — recedes to near-canvas.

radius.$description: Checkbox corner radius — 4px. Square control with just enough softening to read as interactive rather than a data cell.
